description_long: This is a weathered, vertical wooden obelisk set into a patch of earth and surrounded by tufts of green moss or grass. The obelisk is rectangular in cross-section and features visible grain and aging, with a rough, natural texture. One face of the obelisk displays a vertical inscription in white paint, written in Japanese kanji, which stands out against the dark, aged wood. The base is partially embedded in the ground, with some stones and soil visible around it, and the wood appears untreated and exposed to the elements. The tip of the obelisk is flat and unadorned, showing no decorative features. The overall appearance suggests it serves as a marker or signpost, likely in a park or natural setting.
description: A tall, weathered wooden obelisk with Japanese kanji inscription, set in earth and surrounded by moss and stones.
description_view_0: The obelisk is seen from the side, showing its rough wooden texture and the mossy ground at its base.
description_view_1: The front face with a clear vertical Japanese inscription in white paint is visible, along with the mossy and stony base.
description_view_2: The rear side of the obelisk is shown, with no visible inscription and more of the surrounding earth and moss.
description_view_3: A plain side of the obelisk is visible, showing the weathered wood and some moss at the base.
attribute_inscription: Vertical Japanese kanji inscription in white paint on the front face, legible and prominent.
attribute_base_design: The base is simply embedded in the earth, surrounded by moss and some stones, with no decorative features or steps.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat, unadorned, and shows no unique features or decorations.
attribute_surrounding_features: Moss, grass, and some stones are present around the base.
